name: Storyteller Suite

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes in 1.8.13


### Added
- Templates can link the entities they create to **existing** vault entities (locations, magic systems, groups, cultures, etc.) chosen when the template is applied. Define links in a new **Links** tab in the template editor; the apply dialog then prompts for the existing entities to attach. No duplicate entities are created.
- The gallery upload folder is now selected from a dropdown of vault folders instead of a free-text field.

### Fixed
- Plugin settings no longer render blank under newer Obsidian settings windows: the settings layout uses a non-collapsing height, missing settings collections are guarded before rendering, and a failing settings section shows a message instead of blanking the whole pane.

### Internal
- Rolls up ongoing template, timeline, and map editor improvements.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your
vault's `.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/` folder and reload Obsidian.

## Files

- `main.js` - Main plugin file
- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles
releases.name: Release 1.8.13

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes in 1.8.10


### Internal
- Cleared the remaining Obsidian plugin-review lint findings: the timeline dependency-arrow layer now uses `activeDocument` for popout-window compatibility, its static SVG styling moved out of inline assignments into stylesheet classes (with `--dashed`/`--dotted` modifiers for line types), `hasOwnProperty` is called via `Object.prototype`, and a ternary-as-statement became an explicit `if`/`else`. Removed redundant type assertions in the save-as-template, prompt, and dashboard code by narrowing through `const` captures. No user-facing behavior change.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your
vault's `.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/` folder and reload Obsidian.

## Files

- `main.js` - Main plugin file
- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles
releases.name: Release 1.8.10

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes in 1.8.9


### Fixed
- Story Board: the create/overwrite confirmation no longer resolves on a 100ms timeout race. It now awaits the modal result, and creating a new story board opens the generated canvas automatically.
- Groups dashboard: long group descriptions render as a clamped markdown preview instead of overflowing the card.
- Maps dashboard: added an **Open maps panel** action and per-map open buttons so maps are reachable directly from the dashboard.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your
vault's `.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/` folder and reload Obsidian.

## Files

- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles
releases.name: Release 1.8.9

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes in 1.8.8


### Security
- The bundled JSZip dependency no longer ships the `immediate`/`setimmediate` IE-era scheduler fallbacks that injected `<script>` elements at runtime. A build-time transform collapses those four branches to their existing `setTimeout` path, so `main.js` contains no dynamic script-element creation. EPUB/ODT import is unaffected — the `MutationObserver` scheduler JSZip actually uses in Obsidian is left intact.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your
vault's `.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/` folder and reload Obsidian.

## Files

- `main.js` - Main plugin file
- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles
releases.name: Release 1.8.8

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes in 1.8.7


### Fixed
- Map panels now apply the dynamic viewport height (`dvh`) via an `@supports` fallback instead of a duplicate `max-height` declaration, clearing Obsidian's duplicate-property CSS lint warnings.

### Security
- Character-sheet previews now strip inline event handlers (`on*`) and `javascript:` URLs in addition to `<script>`/`<iframe>`/`<object>`/`<embed>`, so user-authored sheet templates cannot execute code.

### Internal
- Release workflow verifies that `manifest.json`/`package.json`/`versions.json` agree with the release tag before building, preventing mismatched release artifacts (the cause of the mis-tagged 1.8.6).

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your
vault's `.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/` folder and reload Obsidian.

## Files

- `main.js` - Main plugin file
- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles
releases.name: Release 1.8.7

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes in 1.8.5


### Fixed
- SceneModal Date field and Campaign board map dropdown no longer overflow past the left edge of their setting row. The plugin-scope `.setting-item-control` rule now constrains its input/select/dropdown children with `max-width: 100%; min-width: 0; box-sizing: border-box` instead of clipping the container with `overflow: hidden`, so wide inputs shrink to fit rather than spilling out and overlapping the label.

### Internal
- Release workflow no longer builds or uploads the `storyteller-suite-X.Y.Z.zip` artifact. Releases now ship only `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` — the three files Obsidian actually loads.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your
vault's `.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/` folder and reload Obsidian.

## Files

- `main.js` - Main plugin file
- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles

releases.name: Release 1.8.2

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
## 1.8.1 

### Changed
- Reduced `!important` declarations in styles by 107 (288 → 181). Remaining instances are necessary vendor overrides for Leaflet, vis-timeline, and Obsidian core styles.
- Expanded all 42 three-digit hex colors to the 6-digit format for consistency.
- Replaced `:has()` selectors in the gallery markdown rendering with explicit classes applied by a markdown post-processor, avoiding the selector-invalidation performance pitfall on large notes.
- Replaced `:has()` selectors used for mobile modal button-container sizing with explicit classes.

### Internal
- Dropped `js-yaml`, `dotenv`, `@types/js-yaml`, and `builtin-modules` from devDependencies (none were used in the bundled output). The build's Node-builtins list is now hardcoded.
- Release workflow now produces GitHub artifact attestations via `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2` for `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`, and the release zip.
- Fixed an `obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case` ESLint error in the Storyteller guide modal ("Ko-fi" → "ko-fi").

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
## Highlights

This release stabilizes the dashboard mobile experience, fixes a hard crash in the network graph, and bundles the ESLint cleanup needed for the Obsidian community plugin scorecard re-grade.

## Fixed
- **Network graph no longer crashes** with `TypeError: s.toLowerCase is not a function`. The relationship resolver now tolerates non-string entries — typed-relationship objects, wikilink-wrapped values, numbers — instead of killing the whole graph when a single field is the wrong shape.
- **Network graph init errors are now logged** to the console instead of being silently swallowed. If the graph ever fails to load again, the actual error and stack will appear.
- **Removed invalid vis-timeline option** (`verticalScrollSticky`) that produced an "Unknown option" warning on every timeline render.
- **Dashboard refresh lifecycle**: stable refresh coordinator replaces the older debounced approach, eliminating duplicated entity views when the dashboard view is reopened or refreshed.

## Changed
- **Ko-fi support button** added to the Storyteller guide modal alongside Dashboard / What's new / Close.
- Updated "What's new" guide content to cover the 1.8.0 dashboard mobile improvements.

## Internal
- Fixed all ESLint violations flagged by the Obsidian community plugin scorecard.
- Tightened type guards in the relationship resolver and across the dashboard refresh path.

## Install
Update via Obsidian's Community plugins panel once the listing refreshes, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below and drop them into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes in 1.7.9-beta.1


- Migrate Culture and Economy modals onto the shared `createStructuredModalLayout` + `createFooterButton` footer used by every other entity modal, so their Save / Cancel / Delete buttons match the rest of the suite on mobile (sticky footer, correct touch targets) instead of rendering as a stacked legacy `Setting` row.

## Installation

1. Download the `storyteller-suite-1.7.9-beta.1.zip` file
2. Extract it to your Obsidian vault's `.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/` folder
3. Restart Obsidian or reload the plugin

## Files

- `main.js` - Main plugin file
- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles (if present)
releases.name: Release 1.7.9-beta.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes in 1.7.9-beta.0


- Auto-update gallery records and entity image references when an image file or its parent folder is renamed or moved, so `profileImagePath`, `coverImagePath`, `backgroundImagePath`, `image`, and `images[]` no longer go stale.
- Read scene Beats, culture/economy/compendium body sections, book Synopsis, and item Cultural Significance / Magic Properties back into their fields when loading notes, so data entered in the markdown body or saved through the modal is no longer dropped on reload.
- Fix scene save/load mismatch where the modal wrote `## Beats` but the loader only read `## Beat Sheet`, then keep `## Beat Sheet` working as a legacy fallback.
- Unify the Cancel / Save / Delete footer on MagicSystem, CompendiumEntry, and TemplateEditor modals so they match the structured-modal layout used across the rest of the suite.
- Restore characters dropped from the writing heatmap when scene `linkedCharacters` capitalisation drifted from the canonical name.
- Include undated events in the analytics events stat, and divide the event-distribution chart by the dated event count so the percentages add up correctly.
- Stop matching unrelated entities by partial-substring during EntitySyncService lookups; fall back only to case-insensitive name matches.
- Run wiki-link bracket canonicalisation through the bidirectional-link backfill so re-imports leave files in the same shape a normal save would.
- Consolidate three duplicate `stripWikiLinkValue` implementations into a shared utility that handles aliases (`[[Real|Display]]`) and anchors (`[[Name#Section]]`).
- Replace the silently lossy fallback frontmatter parser with a strict `parseYaml`-only reader that errors on malformed YAML instead of dropping nested fields.
- Re-run the bidirectional-link backfill on every plugin upgrade instead of only on first install.

## Installation

1. Download the `storyteller-suite-1.7.9-beta.0.zip` file
2. Extract it to your Obsidian vault's `.obsidian/plugins/storyteller-suite/` folder
3. Restart Obsidian or reload the plugin

## Files

- `main.js` - Main plugin file
- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles (if present)
releases.name: Release 1.7.9-beta.0

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
## Storyteller Suite 1.7.0

Big one this time
  ### New: campaign and DnD mode: Kinda felt like a bunch of people were uin
  - Added full campaign sessions with party state, inventory, HP, conditions, flags, revealed lore, and faction standing
  - Added campaign commands for opening sessions, resuming the latest session, starting from the current scene, and
  logging play
  - Added richer scene branch logic so branches can react to items, flags, compendium reveals, and faction standing
  - Added advanced item effects for things like HP changes, conditions, scene travel, lore reveals, and faction standing
  changes

  ### New: character sheet presets
  - Added new DnD-themed character sheet presets
  - Added note-native markdown and callout-based presets for people who want cleaner Obsidian-style output
  - Improved character sheet preview and save flows

  ### Timeline and Gantt overhaul
  - Reworked the Timeline and Gantt experience to feel cleaner and more stable
  - Improved grouped lanes, progress rendering, milestone filtering, dependency arrows, and dependency persistence
  - Fixed a bunch of scroll and rendering problems that were making the timeline feel clunky

  ### Maps and campaign boards
  - Tied image maps into campaign play as actual boards instead of just static references
  - Improved pin, popup, and marker interaction on denser maps
  - Fixed board item pickup persistence in sessions
  - Added SVG support in 2 modes:
    - direct overlay for lighter SVGs
    - rasterized tiled mode for larger SVGs

  ### Compile and writing improvements
  - Expanded compile workflows and custom compile step support
  - Improved draft and workflow handling for manuscript output
  - Tightened scene / draft behavior around new files and draft prompts

  ### Entity system and note syncing
  - Group notes now behave like proper note-backed entities
  - Improved sync between notes and plugin state
  - Improved wiki-link friendly properties so linked entities show up correctly in Properties / graph workflows
  - Unified a lot of the custom field behavior across entity modals

  ### Gallery improvements
  - Added multi-image upload support
  - Improved gallery folder sync and managed-folder discovery
  - Tightened gallery rendering and picker behavior

  ### Mobile, onboarding, and docs
  - Added a real first-run getting started guide
  - Added an in-app update highlights popup for new releases
  - Updated docs and tutorial content to match the current feature set
  - Improved mobile dashboard behavior and cleaned up a bunch of cramped UI spots

  ### Fixes
  - Fixed chapter creation issues with custom folders
  - Fixed text/body-backed fields not saving correctly
  - Fixed custom field modal weirdness
  - Fixed several map and campaign board bugs
  - Fixed a bunch of timeline, gallery, and dashboard regressions found during the launch pass

  If you’ve been using the plugin already, def check updated timeline/Gantt, and the new
  character sheet presets first. I felt like a bunch of people were using SS to catalog D&D campaigns so I tried to make it as interactive as I could in our beloved note taking app lol happy writing.
releases.name: 1.7.0 DnD and bug squashing

releases.author: Maws7140

  New Feature:

  - Useful for users with custom folder structures

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
## Version 1.5.6 - Documentation Updates

### Documentation and Tutorial Improvements
- Comprehensive updates to tutorial sections covering all available features
- Added detailed tutorials for References, Chapters & Scenes, and Maps management
- Enhanced file structure documentation with complete entity folder listings
- Improved custom folders tutorial with full entity type coverage

### Removed Features
- Removed deprecated Calendar entity type (no longer supported)
- Removed all calendar-related configuration and settings
- Cleaned up calendar references from documentation
releases.name: Map update documentation patch

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
### New: Map System
- Introducing map support - place any entity type directly on maps (cultures, economies, magic systems, groups, scenes, references)
- Automatic coordinate storage in entity frontmatter when placing entities on maps
- Entities with map coordinates are automatically discovered and displayed on map views
- Support for entities appearing on multiple maps with different coordinates

### Entity Relationship Sync System
- Automatic bidirectional relationship syncing - update one side, the other updates automatically
- Works across all entity types (characters, locations, events, items, scenes, cultures, economies, magic systems)
- Handles edge cases: name changes, missing entities, prevents circular updates, cleans up stale references
releases.name: Map update!!!

## Code Quality and Maintenance:

- Removal of obsolete documentation
- Code cleanup and organization improvements

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
Smart Content Merging: The plugin now reads existing markdown files before making changes
Section Preservation: Uses regex parsing to extract and preserve existing content from all sections
Selective Updates: Only updates sections that are actually being modified, leaving others untouched
Fallback Protection: If new content is provided, it's used; otherwise, existing content is preserved

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
Storyteller Suite v1.0.3

Organize your fictional world within Obsidian! This initial release introduces the Storyteller Suite dashboard, allowing you to:

Create and manage detailed profiles for Characters and Locations.
Build out your story's timeline by adding Events.
Upload and link images through the integrated Gallery.
Keep everything connected and easily accessible in your vault.
I made this quickly I'll probably other cool stuff I think of

releases.description: I've implemented all requested changes based on the automated review

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
Storyteller Suite v1.0.1

Organize your fictional world within Obsidian! This initial release introduces the Storyteller Suite dashboard, allowing you to:

Create and manage detailed profiles for Characters and Locations.
Build out your story's timeline by adding Events.
Upload and link images through the integrated Gallery.

releases.author: Maws7140
releases.description:
Storyteller Suite v1.0.0

Organize your fictional world within Obsidian! This initial release introduces the Storyteller Suite dashboard, allowing you to:

* Create and manage detailed profiles for Characters and Locations.
* Build out your story's timeline by adding Events.
* Upload and link images through the integrated Gallery.
* Keep everything connected and easily accessible in your vault.

I made this quickly I'll probably other cool stuff I think of.
releases.name: v1.0.0 - Initial Release
